title: eriogonum pusillum
intro: Eriogonum pusillum, also known as the dwarf buckwheat or rice flower, is a perennial wildflower that belongs to the Polygonaceae family. Native to the western United States and Canada, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Native American medicine. The roots and leaves of Eriogonum pusillum have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and is often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
